title: Fixing the LinkedList Insert at Head

statement.markdown: Given a LinkedList and an integer value, insert the value at the head of the LinkedList. The function should not use recursion.
statement.inputFormat: The function takes two parameters, the head of the LinkedList and an integer value.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return the head of the updated LinkedList.
statement.notes: The LinkedList is represented as a series of nodes, where each node has a value and a reference to the next node.

examples.input: Example input (matching the stdin format that main wrapper expects)

hints.text: Consider using a dummy node to simplify the insertion process

functionSignature.java: public ListNode insertAtHead(ListNode head, int value);
functionSignature.python: def insertAtHead(self, head: ListNode, value: int) -> ListNode:

testCases.input: First line count, second line elements (stdin match)
